mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
The Google Cloud SDK provides the command-line tools needed to interact with GKE.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

```

> This command installs the Google Cloud SDK.
> Reload the shell to access gcloud command.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configures kubectl to use the new GKE cluster by updating the kubeconfig file.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Checks the status of the nodes in the GKE cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring for your GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

```

> Enables cluster autoscaling.
> Enables monitoring with Stackdriver.
commands: gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This command deletes the cluster without prompting for confirmation.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your workloads, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json > backup.json

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current workloads.
> Upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that nodes are upgraded and in 'Ready' status.
commands: gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version 1.31 --zone us-central1-a

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that the cluster is not reachable or kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the requested pods.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and consider scaling the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means there are not enough resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Use 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name>' to check events and scale the cluster if needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image is not accessible or does not exist.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure it is available in the specified container registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node types and improved integration with Anthos.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API versions for certain resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure that all workloads are compatible with the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a --num-nodes 3 --cluster-version 1.31
gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes 1 --max-nodes 5 --zone us-central1-a
gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver --zone us-central1-a

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and configured.
